channel.title: NPR Topics: Business
channel.description: Find the latest business news with reports on Wall Street, interest rates, banking, companies, and U.S. and world financial markets. Subscribe to the Business Story of the Day podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Arizona AG files criminal charges against Kalshi over 'illegal gambling'
item.description: Arizona is the first state to allege the prediction market company has committed criminal violations, accusing it of running an unlicensed gambling operation.
item.content:encoded:
Arizona is the first state to allege the prediction market company has committed criminal violations, accusing it of running an unlicensed gambling operation.

(Image credit: Scott Olson)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: Judge orders 1,000 Voice of America staffers back to work in rebuke to Kari Lake
item.description: A federal judge has ordered more than a thousand Voice of America staffers back to work by Monday. It's a major defeat for the Trump administration's effort to cut the news outlet to the bones.
item.content:encoded:
A federal judge has ordered more than a thousand Voice of America staffers back to work by Monday. It's a major defeat for the Trump administration's effort to cut the news outlet to the bones.

(Image credit: SAUL LOEB/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: Is there a more fair way to sell World Cup tickets?
item.description: World Cup tickets are expensive, and buying them has been frustrating and confusing. But this is what economics is for: figuring out the best ways to allocate scarce resources. FIFA, steal these ideas.
item.content:encoded: World Cup tickets are expensive, and buying them has been frustrating and confusing. But this is what economics is for: figuring out the best ways to allocate scarce resources. FIFA, steal these ideas.
item.dc:creator: Alex Mayyasi

item.title: Gasoline prices are still rising as the Iran war stretches into its third week
item.description: U.S. gasoline prices are up nearly 80 cents from a month ago, while diesel prices have shot up even more. Diesel is now just under $5 a gallon, according to AAA, up $1.34 from last month.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. gasoline prices are up nearly 80 cents from a month ago, while diesel prices have shot up even more. Diesel is now just under $5 a gallon, according to AAA, up $1.34 from last month.

(Image credit: Lindsey Wasson)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: Congress and the White House are taking different paths to address housing prices
item.description: Both the Senate and President Trump have taken action recently to address the high cost of housing. But many different economic realities are colliding in the housing market.
item.content:encoded: Both the Senate and President Trump have taken action recently to address the high cost of housing. But many different economic realities are colliding in the housing market.
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: Pentagon tightens controls over Stars and Stripes after calling it "woke"
item.description: The new rules for the independent military newspaper are the Defense Department's latest effort to put extraordinary restrictions on journalists covering the agency.
item.content:encoded:
The new rules for the independent military newspaper are the Defense Department's latest effort to put extraordinary restrictions on journalists covering the agency.

(Image credit: AMEL EMRIC)
item.dc:creator: Maria Aspan

item.title: Can you write off a pet dog? How to vet tax advice from social media
item.description: So you heard a piece of tax advice from a friend or on social media that sounds interesting. Should you try it? A certified public accountant explains how to vet the claim — and avoid getting scammed.
item.content:encoded: So you heard a piece of tax advice from a friend or on social media that sounds interesting. Should you try it? A certified public accountant explains how to vet the claim — and avoid getting scammed.
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra

item.title: Judge blocks DOJ's criminal probe of Federal Reserve, blasting it as political
item.description: A federal judge has put the brakes on a criminal probe of the Federal Reserve, saying it was part of an improper campaign by the Trump administration to pressure the central bank into cutting interest rates.
item.content:encoded:
A federal judge has put the brakes on a criminal probe of the Federal Reserve, saying it was part of an improper campaign by the Trump administration to pressure the central bank into cutting interest rates.

(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Bucking stigma, more places turn to factory-built for affordable housing
item.description: Mobile homes have long been zoned out of cities and suburbs. But with updated designs and a housing shortage, they're increasingly being welcomed as more-affordable starter homes.
item.content:encoded:
Mobile homes have long been zoned out of cities and suburbs. But with updated designs and a housing shortage, they're increasingly being welcomed as more-affordable starter homes.

(Image credit: Anusha Mathur)
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Ludden

item.title: Desperate for skilled workers, a furniture maker looks to apprenticeships for relief
item.description: President Trump has touted apprenticeships as part of his promise of a golden era for American workers. But are his administration's investments enough?
item.content:encoded:
President Trump has touted apprenticeships as part of his promise of a golden era for American workers. But are his administration's investments enough?

(Image credit: Joshua Danquah Asante for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu
